justification: All pass criteria met: (1) Zero-downtime migration via dual-write + CDC backfill with Strangler Fig pattern. (2) Phases clearly defined: schema setup, backfill, dual-write, shadow reads, read cutover, reverse dual-write, decommission. (3) Rollback plan at each phase via feature flag phase controller and reverse dual-write during cutover. (4) MongoDB data shape inconsistency explicitly called out as critical risk with schema analysis mitigation. (5) Trade-offs include dual-write latency impact and operational overhead of maintaining two stores.

observations.text: Exceptionally thorough treatment of MongoDB schema inconsistency risk — elevated to critical severity with concrete mitigation (schema analysis job, dead-letter queue for unmappable documents)
Shadow read verification phase adds confidence-building step beyond the basic dual-write pattern
Reverse dual-write during Phase 4 ensures MongoDB stays current for rollback — addresses the subtle risk of data loss on rollback
Backfill design is production-safe: reads from secondary replica, throttled, resumable via high-water mark, idempotent via ON CONFLICT upserts
Quantitative gate for cutover (99.99% match rate sustained 24 hours) prevents premature migration
Identified timing-based false mismatches in shadow reads and proposed staleness window tolerance
Trade-off analysis explicitly rejected 2PC and big-bang alternatives with clear reasoning

agent_output_excerpt:
{
  "executive_summary": "Zero-downtime migration of the Order Management domain from MongoDB to PostgreSQL using a phased Strangler Fig approach with dual-write, shadow-read verification, and incremental cutover. The architecture introduces a Data Access Abstraction Layer (DAAL) to decouple application code from storage, a Change Data Capture (CDC) pipeline for historical backfill, and a dual-write coordinator for real-time synchronization.
